name: Panevezys County (Lithuania)

colors.symbolism: the outer field of Lithuanian county flags

description: A blue field with the coat of arms of Panevezys County in the centre, set within an inner panel framed by the standard Lithuanian-county blue border.
history: Like the flags of all ten Lithuanian counties, Panevezys County's flag follows the uniform template introduced after the country's redivision into apskritys (counties) in 1994: a blue field surrounding the county's heraldic device.

features.symbolism: blue outer border in the Lithuanian county pattern, surrounding the county's heraldic field

features.role: coat of arms of Panevezys County
features.symbolism: a stylised gateway and other emblems on a silver field, referencing the county's regional and historical character
